sectionOrderRules: Hero must include what kinds of machines or equipment the company works with, optional location if known, and a primary contact CTA.
Service summary must describe concrete work such as repair, preventive maintenance and installation, not vague all-round claims.
If emergency-service is shown, it must state the actual availability only when the dossier supports it.
Trust proof must appear before the final contact CTA and should focus on real signals such as certificates, service agreements, response routines or documented experience.
Sections should avoid repeating the same promise in multiple blocks.
Services intro must connect the company to repair, maintenance and installation work in a specific and practical way.
Service list items must be concrete examples of work performed on machines, equipment or production-related systems.
Pricing-note may explain how quotes, inspections or call-out fees work, but must not invent fixed prices if none are known.
If trust-proof is included, it should support the service offer with real qualifications or process clarity, not duplicate the intro.
Service-agreements intro must explain who the agreements are for and why preventive maintenance matters.
Agreement-benefits should focus on practical outcomes such as reduced downtime, planned service intervals and clearer follow-up.
Agreement-scope must describe examples of included checks, maintenance visits or documentation routines without promising more than the company can deliver.
The page must end with a CTA to discuss needs, book a review or request a quote.
About-story must be specific about the company's background, focus or way of working, not generic quality wording.
Team should emphasize relevant technician roles, experience areas or service approach when data exists.
Trust-proof should use real evidence such as certifications, documented routines, response readiness or long-term customer relationships.
Contact-info must include available contact methods supplied by the business and must not use placeholder data.
If opening-hours is shown, it must reflect actual staffed hours or service availability.
Contact CTA should support both urgent service inquiries and planned maintenance or installation requests.
